title: 1000-plan library PLAN record v1 (PROPOSED)
description: A single PLAN record in the 1000-plan library (plan-factory/PLAN_LIBRARY_SEED_1000_v1.json). DESIGN ONLY: the library validator that enforces this schema is TO BE BUILT (phase PF-B); nothing here claims a built, active, or verified component. A plan is a reusable template: instantiated per order into a standard p0_prompt_packet_v1.1 packet wrapped by a client_order_to_factory_run_v0 envelope. This schema never mutates P0-PGR schemas or the cloud-factory v0 package (no_authority_change gate; version law: v1 amends and wins only on direct conflict, v0 stays live). Order rule 5 is structural here: every plan carries cost_cap_usd, authority_scope, sandbox_target, and receipt_schema as REQUIRED fields — a plan missing any of them is invalid, not merely incomplete.
laws: FOUNDER_CANON v1 + governed-autorun v3. Violations = BLOCKED_WITH_REASON.

plan_id.description: PLAN-0001..PLAN-1000. Ids are banded 100 per plan_type in fixed order: app_builder 0001-0100, workflow_builder 0101-0200, research 0201-0300, compliance_governance 0301-0400, deploy 0401-0500, verification 0501-0600, revenue 0601-0700, client_onboarding 0701-0800, site_app_repair 0801-0900, automation_cron 0901-1000. Band maps (default line plans + risk defaults) live in plan-factory/PLAN_LIBRARY_SEED_1000_v1.json (bands); a fuller amendment registry exists only as the unratified addendum draft FACTORY_LINE_REGISTRY_v1.json.

plan_version.description: Semver. Immutable once status is active (prompt-registry precedent: version immutable once activated; two versions never active at once). Changing an active plan means a new version record, never an in-place edit.

plan_type.description: Exactly 10 plan types, one 100-id band each (order fixed by founder order). Type-specific laws: deploy plans are preview-only (production stays FOUNDER_ONLY outside the substrate; HARD_BLOCK reason 2); revenue plans produce offers/funnels as DRAFTS only — no sends, no pricing claims without founder; automation_cron plans compile + preview ONLY — activating any schedule is double-locked (FOUNDER-UNLOCK-CF-CRON + per-workflow founder activation; recurring_copilot_automation is forbidden today).

venture.description: Owning venture for the plan template. client = plan built for external client orders via the client workspace product (CF-P2+, NOT BUILT). Client authority never exceeds the client's own run scope.

mission_template.description: Mission text with {{placeholders}} filled from the order at pipeline stage 3 (Instantiate — PLAN_FACTORY_ARCHITECTURE_v1.md §4, the canonical pipeline). The instantiated mission becomes the packet mission and must pass the existing R1-R9 packet lint unchanged (R6 bans vague-verb missions without measurable acceptance; R7 bans routing validation/review/uncertainty to the founder; lint fail files a repair candidate and the lane continues — never default to STOP).

line_plan.description: Ordered factory line ids (cloud-factory/FACTORY_LINE_REGISTRY_v0.json rows — the resolution registry; plan-aware band maps in plan-factory/PLAN_LIBRARY_SEED_1000_v1.json). Must be composable per the v0 consumes/produces chain and a subset of the plan_type default line-plan spine (or justified in notes). Every plan run resolves its line_plan against the registry; a plan naming a line not in the registry is a lint reject. Order rule 6 anchor: every execution runs through a cloud line or a controlled sandbox — line_plan resolution plus the sandbox cell ledger, never ad-hoc execution.

cost_cap_usd.description: Per-run cost cap in USD (default 5.00; per-line default 2.00 inherited from v0). Breach halts the line, writes a PARTIAL receipt, and sends the run to QUEUED_FOUNDER_REVIEW (FT-CAPITAL). An order budget may LOWER this cap, never raise it above the plan cap without founder approval (pipeline stage 3 rule). Costs roll up: line receipt -> run receipt -> phase2 scorecard counters.cost_usd. Order rule 5 anchor: every plan has a cost cap.

receipt_schema.description: Plans reuse the v0 receipt schemas unchanged: line receipts receipts/factory/<FR-ID>/<line_key>-<ISO8601Z>.json (factory_line_receipt_v0), run receipt receipts/factory/<FR-ID>-run-<ISO8601Z>.json (factory_run_receipt_v0), both carrying all NINE P0-PGR gates, quality_state, evidence[], cost, with machine-generated timestamps (never typed). A plan may ADD a plan_receipt_note (optional field) appended to its receipts but never removes receipt fields. No receipt sink = no line (governed-autorun L8/L11). Order rule 5 anchor: every plan has a receipt schema.

plan_receipt_note.description: OPTIONAL. Note appended to this plan's line/run receipts. Additive only — never removes or overrides factory_line_receipt_v0 / factory_run_receipt_v0 fields.

risk_class.description: Defaults by plan_type (overridable per plan; map plan_type_risk_defaults in plan-factory/PLAN_LIBRARY_SEED_1000_v1.json bands): research/verification = low; workflow_builder/app_builder/client_onboarding/site_app_repair = medium; deploy/revenue/compliance_governance/automation_cron = high. Order rule 7 anchor: high-risk transitions — anything touching production, spend, sends, legal/equity/payment claims, schedule activation, or governance zones — REQUIRE founder/client approval via authority_scope.approvals_required. PLAN_RANKING_ROI_MODEL_v1 risk_penalty: low 0, medium -5, high -15.

status.description: seed = shipped in plan-factory/PLAN_LIBRARY_SEED_1000_v1.json, not yet founder-ratified. Growth law: plans enter as draft via FL10 harvest or architect order; a founder receipt ratifies seed -> testing -> active; plan_version is immutable once active; retired plans are never deleted (rejection is never permission to delete). No self-promotion to active (RF1/RF2/RF3 fence precedent: status flips are founder-receipted, never machine-claimed).

inputs.description: Artifact types the plan consumes, from the v0 line consumes/produces chain. Must be satisfiable by the order intake plus upstream lines in line_plan.

outputs.description: Artifact types the plan produces, from the v0 line consumes/produces chain. Every artifact gets its sha256 recorded in its line receipt.

evidence_required.description: Claims that must carry evidence in the run's receipts (evidence kinds: url_fetch | script_run | file_stat | hash). Receipt provenance law: every external claim needs per-URL status + body sha256 + saved body under the run's artifact dir. FL8 verification PASS only from a secondary CF account edge receipt — never builder self-report (autonomy loop L3).

notes.description: Free-form plan notes (required field; may be empty). A line_plan deviating from its band's default spine must be justified here.

sandbox_target.description: Order rule 5 anchor: every plan names its sandbox target. Cells are allocated by the sandbox manager (cloud-factory/SANDBOX_MANAGER_SPEC_v0.md base law + the execution-cell delta in PLAN_FACTORY_ARCHITECTURE_v1.md §7, TO BE BUILT); walls W1-W9 enforced as mechanical exit checks, not mid-run permission loops.

repo.description: Target repo for cell allocation. Venture deliverables allocate cells in the venture repo (one actor · one repo · one scope); SG repo cells only for governance artifacts; cross-repo runs take one cell per repo, one writer per cell, run envelope references all cells.

sandbox_required.description: When true, no line of this plan executes without an allocated cell (ledger row). One writer per task cell; a second writer gets a REJECTED receipt (CAS per PARALLEL_AUTOMATION L1).

env_profile.description: Deny-by-default secret policy (v0): raw secrets never enter the worktree environment; execution requiring a secret runs where the secret already lives. Build/code lines get NO send-capable credentials — makes the no_external_send gate structural.

model_policy.description: Order rule 4 anchor: no premium models unless ROI justifies — carried by premium_rule and enforced structurally by the PLAN_RANKING_ROI_MODEL_v1 premium_model_penalty.

default_tier.description: Default model tier for the plan's lines (v0 registry model_tier enum). PLAN_RANKING_ROI_MODEL_v1 applies premium_model_penalty -10 when default_tier is judgment AND score_base < 60.

premium_rule.default: judgment tier allowed only if roi_priors weighted score >= 60 or an explicit founder order names it
premium_rule.description: Law-bearing rule string (order rule 4): the judgment tier is allowed ONLY if the roi_priors weighted score (35/25/15/15/10 base model, max 100) is >= 60 OR an explicit founder order names it. No plan may weaken this rule — a weaker premium_rule is a lint reject (never weaken a gate; fix the ROI case instead).

authority_scope.description: Order rule 5 anchor: every plan has an authority scope. allowed_scope feeds the sandbox cell's allowed_scope at instantiation; forbidden_actions carry the fixed structural bans; approvals_required carries the human gates (same shape as the v0 envelope approvals_required).

allowed_scope.description: Path globs the plan's cells may write. Copied into sandbox allowed_scope at pipeline stage 3; out-of-scope writes fail the W9 wall at cell exit. Governance/SSOT/ledger paths (W2) are out of scope unless explicitly listed.

forbidden_actions.description: MUST include the five fixed entries (schema-enforced via contains): direct_main_write (order rule 1 — repo fences + CODEOWNERS + sandbox branches + W1 wall), external_send (order rule 2 — no_external_send gate + no send-capable credentials in cells), production_deploy (FL7 is preview-only; HARD_BLOCK reason 2), legal_equity_payment_claims (order rule 3 — no_legal_financial_commitment gate + claim ladder VERIFIED/DECLARED/PLANNED), cron_schedule (scheduled automation is FOUNDER_ONLY; automation_cron plans compile + preview only, activation double-locked FOUNDER-UNLOCK-CF-CRON + per-workflow founder activation). Plans may ADD further forbidden actions, never remove these five.

approvals_required.description: Same shape as v0 envelope approvals_required (client_order_to_factory_run_v0). Order rule 7 anchor: founder rows are mandatory for any scope touching production, spend, sends, legal/equity/payment claims, schedule activation, or governance zones; client rows gate only the client's own run's preview-to-deliver step and never exceed their run scope. Every founder decision writes a receipt with verbatim scope (M03 lesson: chat-only authorization is not durable evidence).

scope.description: verbatim scope this approval must cover

roi_priors.description: Five ROI prior axes, each scored 0-5 — the SAME five axes and 35/25/15/15/10 weights as scripts/p0pgr_phase2_rank_v1.py (score_base = sum(weight * clamp(axis,0,5)/5), max 100). PLAN_RANKING_ROI_MODEL_v1 applies bounded deterministic modifiers ON TOP of this base; the plan ranker NEVER invents a new base model and consumes the SAME queue (receipts/p0pgr/phase2_queue_v1.json), never a private one.

auto_dispatch.description: Order rule 8 anchor: auto-dispatch is per-template only and arrives LATER (PF-E). eligible is const false in v1 for every plan, including low-risk ones.

eligible.description: const false in v1. Low-risk templates can LATER be founder-approved per template. The PLAN_RANKING_ROI_MODEL_v1 auto_dispatch_gate (risk_class == low AND reuse_bonus == 5, i.e. >= 5 verified runs with FL8 external PASS, AND zero wall violations in those runs AND cost under cap in all runs) computes ELIGIBILITY only — dispatch approval is a founder receipt per template, never a machine flip. Auto-dispatch never unlocks scheduling: cron_schedule stays in forbidden_actions regardless.

unlock_rule.const: per-template founder receipt naming the plan_id + plan_version; never class-wide
unlock_rule.description: Fixed unlock law (const to prevent drift): eligibility may only flip via a per-template founder receipt in receipts/p0pgr/founder/ naming this exact plan_id + plan_version. Never class-wide, never chat approval (autonomy expands only on receipt streaks plus founder receipt — L8 earned-autonomy pattern).

artifact_type.description: Artifact-type vocabulary from the v0 factory line consumes/produces chain. preview_artifact is preview-only (pr | gh_artifact | preview_url | dry_run_trace); production deploy artifacts do not exist in this substrate.
